summary: Enhanced meeting_depositor.py with video expiry tracking, full Recall metadata persistence, improved title extraction, recording duration calculation, and manifest v2.0 schema with all new fields.
learnings: Recall's meeting_metadata.title is the best source for meeting titles as it comes directly from the calendar integration. The 180-day video retention means we should warn users about expiry.
concerns: Video files will expire after 180 days - no archival strategy is included. Users should be aware of this limitation.

decisions.decision: Use manifest_version: '2.0' to indicate enhanced schema
decisions.rationale: New fields (video_expires_at, participant_count, recording_duration_seconds) represent a significant schema change that consumers should be aware of

decisions.decision: Save raw bot response to recall_metadata.json
decisions.rationale: Preserves full API response for debugging, audit trail, and future feature development without data loss

decisions.decision: Calculate video expiry from bot creation date + 180 days
decisions.rationale: Recall's retention policy is fixed at 180 days; calculating expiry at deposit time gives users a clear deadline
Calendar data (meeting_metadata.title) is best but may not always be present; falling back to meeting_url.meeting_id and bot_name ensures robustness

changes: Added extract_meeting_title() function with 5-level fallback for title extraction
Added calculate_recording_duration() to compute duration from status_changes timestamps
Added calculate_video_expiry() to compute 180-day expiry timestamp
Added save_recall_metadata() to persist raw bot response for audit
Updated manifest to v2.0 with video_expires_at, video_retention_days, participant_count, recording_duration_seconds, recall_meeting_id fields
Enhanced logging to warn about video expiry
Updated import to include timedelta for date calculations
